macro_events.event_summary: CNBC reports that mortgage rates are likely to rise further later in 2026, signaling continued pressure on housing affordability.
primary_drivers: Higher mortgage rates reduce housing affordability and purchase volume
Existing homeowners locked into low rates reduce inventory turnover

beneficiaries.rationale: ICE Mortgage Technology benefits from refinancing volume volatility and owns Ellie Mae/Black Knight servicing platforms — higher rates increase demand for servicing analytics as portfolios stay on books longer. Secondary beneficiary only.
Moody's structured finance ratings revenue is somewhat counter-cyclical to housing; rising rates push more activity into non-agency/private credit channels needing ratings. Indirect.
Equity Residential — large-cap multifamily REIT directly benefits from rent-vs-buy math tilting toward renting when mortgage rates rise; coastal urban exposure with limited new supply.

losers.rationale: D.R. Horton is the largest US homebuilder by volume with entry-level buyer concentration — the most rate-sensitive cohort. Higher mortgage rates directly compress order volumes and force more incentive spending (rate buydowns) that crush gross margins.

Lennar has similar entry-level exposure to DHI and already runs aggressive mortgage rate buydown programs through its captive finance arm — rising rates make these buydowns more expensive, directly hitting margins.
Rocket Companies is a pure-play mortgage originator; refinance volume is already depressed and purchase originations fall further as rates climb. Direct revenue hit with high operating leverage.
UWM Holdings is the largest wholesale mortgage lender — gain-on-sale margins compress and volume declines when rates rise, with limited ability to offset via servicing because it sells MSRs.
Zillow's IMT (premier agent) and mortgage segments both directly tied to home transaction volume; higher rates mean fewer transactions, lower lead value, and weaker mortgage attach rates.
Opendoor's iBuying model requires transaction velocity and stable/rising home prices; higher rates slow turnover, raise inventory holding costs, and force markdowns on existing book.
Builders FirstSource — building products supplier with ~40% single-family construction exposure; new home starts decline with mortgage rates, hitting lumber/truss/millwork volumes directly.

headlines: What's the mortgage rate you'll be paying later this year? Probably higher

macro_events.event_summary: Pentagon awards Shield AI a contract for a low-cost drone program as the Iran conflict drives demand for cheap, attritable drones.
primary_drivers: Wartime demand surge for low-cost attritable drones
Pentagon procurement shift toward cheap autonomous systems over exquisite platforms
Validation of AI-enabled autonomy vendors over legacy primes

beneficiaries.rationale: Kratos is the closest US-listed pure-play on low-cost attritable drones (Valkyrie XQ-58, Mako). A Pentagon program validating the cheap-drone thesis directly expands its addressable market and read-across pricing.
AeroVironment is the dominant listed small/tactical drone supplier (Switchblade loitering munitions, Puma). Iran-driven demand for cheap drones directly lifts Switchblade reorders and FMS pipeline.
Red Cat won the Army SRR short-range recon drone program; a Pentagon push toward low-cost drones reinforces its core procurement thesis and peer-rotation flows into small-cap drone names.
Ondas owns American Robotics/Airobotics and is a small-cap drone beneficiary of momentum/peer rotation when Pentagon awards highlight the cheap-drone category.
Palantir provides the AI/autonomy software stack (Maven, TITAN) that pairs with autonomous drone fleets; Shield AI's win validates the AI-defense software thesis Palantir trades on.

Lockheed makes Hellfire/JAGM and partners on loitering munitions; Iran war escalation lifts munitions restock demand even as cheap drones compete with exquisite platforms — net positive on the war angle.

losers.rationale: General Dynamics' exquisite platform mix (combat vehicles, subs) is the implicit budget-share loser when Pentagon dollars rotate toward cheap attritable drones; also competes with Shield AI on some autonomy bids.
Northrop's high-end UAS portfolio (Global Hawk-class, MQ-4C Triton) represents the expensive-drone paradigm the Pentagon is explicitly moving away from with this low-cost program.

headlines: Pentagon taps Shield AI for low-cost drone program as Iran war accelerates demand for cheap drones
